sources.label: Yahoo Finance - EMCOR Group (EME) quote and news
Simply Wall St - EME valuation after recent share price pullback
TipRanks - EME analyst forecasts and price targets

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; the ~8% one-month pullback (incl. -3.3% June 5, -0.9% today) with the data-center MEP thesis intact marginally improves asymmetry, but EME remains a discovered, consensus Strong Buy name far from the passive/power bottleneck core, so rank 98 in the Monitor/benchmark bucket stands.
verdicts.bull: Raised FY26 guidance ($18.5-19.25B revenue) and 20% Q1 revenue growth confirm data-center electrical/mechanical demand is structural; the recent drawdown offers a slightly better entry into a tight skilled-labor franchise.
verdicts.neutral: Quality compounder with real AI-buildout exposure, but at ~$36B cap, +27.8% YTD and a ~71% one-year return it is fully discovered and offers benchmark-like, not hidden, alpha.
verdicts.bear: Post-rerating multiple leaves little residual alpha; any data-center capex digestion or margin normalization in construction segments would hit a crowded, momentum-owned name hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (late July) with backlog/RPO growth and any further guidance raise on data-center demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Hyperscaler capex deceleration or project timing slippage compressing the rerated construction multiple.
